note: Delta pairs from Plan A P0 execution (batches B-01..B-06). Extracted only from the 137 target verbs' newly authored :examples entries. Main reference-examples JSONL is not modified by this file.

messages.content: Write a novice Motoi example that uses `ai/arrive`.

```motoi
(ai/arrive 'hero 100 100)
```

Steer the hero to (100, 100). Slows down as it gets close so it doesn't overshoot.
Show me `ai/arrive` at the intermediate level.

```motoi
(on-frame
  (lambda ()
    (ai/arrive 'hero 200 50 30 4)))
```

Arrive with a 30-unit slow radius and a steering force of 4. Feels smooth.
Write a novice Motoi example that uses `ai/bb-del!`.

```motoi
(ai/bb-set! 'hero 'goal 'chest)
(ai/bb-del! 'hero 'goal)
```

Store a goal, then forget it. The delete returns #t because the key existed.
Show me `ai/bb-del!` at the intermediate level.

```motoi
(when (ai/bb-has? 'hero 'temp)
  (ai/bb-del! 'hero 'temp))
```

Only try to delete a key that's actually there. Defensive tidy-up.
Write a novice Motoi example that uses `ai/bb-get`.

```motoi
(ai/bb-set! 'hero 'goal 'chest)
(display (ai/bb-get 'hero 'goal))
```

Store a goal in the blackboard, then read it back.
Show me `ai/bb-get` at the intermediate level.

```motoi
(display (ai/bb-get 'hero 'mood 'neutral))
```

If mood isn't set, fall back to 'neutral. Default protects against missing keys.
Write a novice Motoi example that uses `ai/bb-has?`.

```motoi
(display (ai/bb-has? 'hero 'goal))
```

Does the hero have a 'goal on the blackboard? True or false.
Show me `ai/bb-has?` at the intermediate level.

```motoi
(when (not (ai/bb-has? 'hero 'goal))
  (ai/bb-set! 'hero 'goal 'wander))
```

Only assign a goal if the hero doesn't already have one.
Write a novice Motoi example that uses `ai/bb-set!`.

```motoi
(ai/bb-set! 'hero 'goal 'chest)
```

Write 'goal = chest into the hero's blackboard.
Show me `ai/bb-set!` at the intermediate level.

```motoi
(on-frame
  (lambda ()
    (ai/bb-set! 'hero 'last-pos (entity/pos 'hero))))
```

Remember the hero's last position each frame. Handy for undo or trailing effects.
Write a novice Motoi example that uses `ai/bt-force`.

```motoi
(define always-ok
  (ai/bt-force 'success 'try-attack))
(display always-ok)
```

Force the child to look successful. Useful when you want the parent tree to keep going.
Show me `ai/bt-force` at the intermediate level.

```motoi
(define keep-trying
  (ai/bt-force 'running 'search))
(display keep-trying)
```

Pin a slow action in running state so higher-level logic knows it's still busy.
Write a novice Motoi example that uses `ai/bt-invert`.

```motoi
(define not-hungry (ai/bt-invert 'hungry?))
(display not-hungry)
```

Wrap a check to flip its meaning. Now the node succeeds when the child fails.
Show me `ai/bt-invert` at the intermediate level.

```motoi
(define at-rest
  (ai/bt-invert (ai/bt-condition 'moving?)))
(display at-rest)
```

Build 'not-moving' from a 'moving?' check without writing a new condition.
Write a novice Motoi example that uses `ai/bt-parallel`.

```motoi
(define both
  (ai/bt-parallel 2 'walk 'chew-gum))
(display both)
```

A quorum of 2 out of 2. Both children must succeed for the parallel to succeed.
Show me `ai/bt-parallel` at the intermediate level.

```motoi
(define any-two
  (ai/bt-parallel 2 'see 'hear 'smell))
(display any-two)
```

Three senses run in parallel. As long as any two report success, the parallel wins.
Write a novice Motoi example that uses `ai/clear!`.

```motoi
(define grid (ai/grid 8 8))
(ai/wall! grid 3 3)
(ai/clear! grid 3 3)
```

Put a wall on a cell, then take it back down. Passable again.
Show me `ai/clear!` at the intermediate level.

```motoi
(define grid (ai/grid 16 16))
(for-each
  (lambda (cell)
    (ai/clear! grid (car cell) (cadr cell)))
  '((0 0) (0 1) (0 2)))
```

Clear a small door out of a wall by opening three adjacent cells.
Write a novice Motoi example that uses `ai/cohere`.

```motoi
(ai/cohere 'bird 'flock 50)
```

Steer this bird toward the middle of nearby flockmates within 50 units.
Show me `ai/cohere` at the intermediate level.

```motoi
(for-each
  (lambda (id)
    (ai/cohere id 'flock 40 2))
  (entity/all))
```

Apply the cohesion rule to every entity in the flock each step. Force of 2 keeps it gentle.
Write a novice Motoi example that uses `ai/decide`.

```motoi
(display (ai/decide 'hero '((attack 0.8) (flee 0.3))))
```

Score each option. The higher score wins. The chosen action fires.
Show me `ai/decide` at the intermediate level.

```motoi
(define (choose hero-id hp-frac)
  (ai/decide hero-id
    (list (list 'attack (- 1 hp-frac))
          (list 'flee   hp-frac))))
(display (choose 'hero 0.2))
```

As health falls, fleeing scores higher. The hero decides for themselves.
Write a novice Motoi example that uses `ai/evade`.

```motoi
(ai/evade 'mouse 'cat)
```

The mouse steers away from where the cat is heading. Not just where the cat is.
Show me `ai/evade` at the intermediate level.

```motoi
(on-frame
  (lambda ()
    (ai/evade 'ship 'meteor 5)))
```

Every frame, the ship steers away from the meteor with a force of 5.
Write a novice Motoi example that uses `ai/flow-at`.

```motoi
(define field (ai/flow-field 8 8 '(4 4)))
(display (ai/flow-at field 0 0))
```

Read the flow direction at cell (0, 0). Returns a small vector.
Show me `ai/flow-at` at the intermediate level.

```motoi
(define field (ai/flow-field 16 16 '(8 8)))
(let ((dir (ai/flow-at field 2 2)))
  (display dir))
```

Ask a 16 by 16 flow field, aiming at (8, 8), where to go from (2, 2).
Write a novice Motoi example that uses `ai/passable?`.

```motoi
(define grid (ai/grid 8 8))
(display (ai/passable? grid 0 0))
```

Fresh grid — every cell is passable. Returns true.
Show me `ai/passable?` at the intermediate level.

```motoi
(define grid (ai/grid 8 8))
(ai/wall! grid 2 2)
(display (ai/passable? grid 2 2))
```

Wall a cell, then ask if it's passable. Now false.
Write a novice Motoi example that uses `ai/utility`.

```motoi
(display (ai/utility 'hero '((sleep 0.2) (eat 0.7))))
```

Two options, two scores. The winner comes back as a tag. Here: 'eat.
Show me `ai/utility` at the intermediate level.

```motoi
(define (score hunger tired)
  (list (list 'eat hunger) (list 'sleep tired)))
(display (ai/utility 'hero (score 0.9 0.4)))
```

Build the scores from actual needs. Hungry beats tired — 'eat wins.
Write a novice Motoi example that uses `ai/waypoints`.

```motoi
(define grid (ai/grid 16 16))
(define wps (ai/waypoints grid '((0 0) (1 0) (2 0))))
(display wps)
```

Turn a three-cell path into world-space waypoints at cell centers.
Show me `ai/waypoints` at the intermediate level.

```motoi
(define grid (ai/grid 32 32))
(define path (ai/path grid '(0 0) '(10 10)))
(define wps (ai/waypoints grid path))
(display wps)
```

Run A* on the grid, then convert the cell path into waypoints an entity can steer through.
Write a novice Motoi example that uses `across-beats`.

```motoi
(across-beats 'line)
```

Make a formation descriptor. Later choreography will use it to place entities.
Show me `across-beats` at the intermediate level.

```motoi
(define row (across-beats 'staggered))
(display row)
```

A staggered formation feels more human than a strict grid. Hand-off row to a mover.
Write a novice Motoi example that uses `after-frame`.

```motoi
(after-frame
  (lambda ()
    (display "paint settled")))
```

Runs once, after the browser has actually painted the frame you just built.
Show me `after-frame` at the intermediate level.

```motoi
(begin-frame 'main)
(grid-cell-set! 3 3 1)
(end-frame 'main)
(after-frame
  (lambda ()
    (display (grid-live-count))))
```

Paint a cell, then measure the live count only after paint has finished.
Write a novice Motoi example that uses `arc-between`.

```motoi
(define path (arc-between '(0 0) '(100 0)))
(display path)
```

A curved path from the origin to (100, 0). Not a straight line.
Show me `arc-between` at the intermediate level.

```motoi
(define swoop (arc-between '(0 0) '(100 100) '(height 40)))
(display swoop)
```

Same arc but 40 units of vertical lift. Reads as a graceful swoop.
Write a novice Motoi example that uses `audio-band`.

```motoi
(if (> (audio-band 2) 0.4)
    (display "kick!")
    (display "."))
```

The lowest bands carry the drum. Print a mark when the kick pushes past 0.4.
Show me `audio-band` at the intermediate level.

```motoi
(define (spring-height)
  (+ 20 (* 100 (audio-band 3))))
(on-frame
  (lambda ()
    (grid-cell-set! 5 5 (spring-height))))
```

Bind a cell's height to a bass band so the grid bounces with the song.
Write a novice Motoi example that uses `audio-bands`.

```motoi
(display (length (audio-bands)))
```

See how many bands the engine offers before you decide how many to draw.
Show me `audio-bands` at the intermediate level.

```motoi
(define (loudest)
  (let loop ((bands (audio-bands)) (i 0) (best 0) (best-i 0))
    (if (null? bands)
        best-i
        (if (> (car bands) best)
            (loop (cdr bands) (+ i 1) (car bands) i)
            (loop (cdr bands) (+ i 1) best best-i)))))
```

Walk the spectrum and remember which band shouted loudest this frame.
Write a novice Motoi example that uses `audio-bar-clock`.

```motoi
(define clk (audio-bar-clock (audio-play 'loop)))
(display (clk 'phase))
```

The clock hands back its phase as a number between 0 and 1 across one bar.
Show me `audio-bar-clock` at the intermediate level.

```motoi
(define clk (audio-bar-clock (audio-play 'song) 4))
(on-frame
  (lambda ()
    (let ((r (clk 'radians)))
      (display r))))
```

A 4-bar clock cycles once every four bars; radians are handy for rotating shapes.
Write a novice Motoi example that uses `audio-beat?`.

```motoi
(on-frame
  (lambda ()
    (when (audio-beat?)
      (grid-cell-set! 0 0 1))))
```

Blink cell (0,0) each time the engine hears a beat.
Show me `audio-beat?` at the intermediate level.

```motoi
(define count 0)
(on-frame
  (lambda ()
    (when (audio-beat?)
      (set! count (+ count 1))
      (when (= (modulo count 4) 0)
        (display "downbeat")))))
```

Count beats and mark every fourth one as a downbeat.
Write a novice Motoi example that uses `audio-bpm`.

```motoi
(let ((bpm (audio-bpm)))
  (if bpm (display bpm) (display "no tempo yet")))
```

Ask the engine for the song's tempo; it may not know yet.
Show me `audio-bpm` at the intermediate level.

```motoi
(define (beat-length-ms)
  (let ((bpm (audio-bpm)))
    (if bpm (/ 60000 bpm) 500)))
(display (beat-length-ms))
```

Turn a tempo into the length of one beat in milliseconds. Fall back to 500 ms.
Write a novice Motoi example that uses `audio-halt`.

```motoi
(define h (audio-play 'chime))
(audio-halt h)
```

Play a chime, then cut it off. Handles are how you talk to sounds already playing.
Show me `audio-halt` at the intermediate level.

```motoi
(define bg (audio-play 'ambient))
(on-key
  (lambda (k)
    (when (eq? k 'q)
      (audio-halt bg))))
```

Press Q to quiet the ambient loop. Useful when a boss battle starts.
Write an intermediate Motoi example that uses `audio-master-volume`.

```motoi
(define (fade-out steps)
  (let loop ((i steps))
    (when (> i 0)
      (audio-master-volume (/ i steps))
      (wait 50)
      (loop (- i 1)))))
```

Step the master volume down to zero for a slow fade at the end of a scene.
Show me `audio-master-volume` at the novice level.

```motoi
(audio-master-volume 0.25)
```

Cut the overall volume to a quarter. Ranges 0.0 (silent) to 1.0 (full).
Write a novice Motoi example that uses `audio-peak`.

```motoi
(display (audio-peak))
```

One number that tells you how loud the loudest sample is this frame.
Show me `audio-peak` at the intermediate level.

```motoi
(on-frame
  (lambda ()
    (let ((p (audio-peak)))
      (grid-cell-set! 0 0 (if (> p 0.9) 1 0)))))
```

Light up a cell whenever the audio is near clipping.
Write a novice Motoi example that uses `audio-perceptual-bands`.

```motoi
(display (length (audio-perceptual-bands)))
```

Perceptual bands weight the spectrum the way our ears actually hear it.
Show me `audio-perceptual-bands` at the intermediate level.

```motoi
(define (bar-heights)
  (map (lambda (b) (* 10 b)) (audio-perceptual-bands)))
(display (bar-heights))
```

Turn the perceptual spectrum into a list of bar heights for a fair-looking visualizer.
Write a novice Motoi example that uses `audio-play`.

```motoi
(audio-play 'ping)
```

Start a sample. The engine returns a handle you can keep if you want to stop it later.
Show me `audio-play` at the intermediate level.

```motoi
(define song (audio-play 'song))
(on-key
  (lambda (k)
    (when (eq? k 'space)
      (if (audio-playing?)
          (audio-halt song)
          (audio-play 'song)))))
```

Space bar toggles the song. If it's playing, stop it; otherwise start it again.
Write a novice Motoi example that uses `audio-playing?`.

```motoi
(if (audio-playing?)
    (display "sound on")
    (display "silence"))
```

Ask the engine whether anything is actually making noise right now.
Show me `audio-playing?` at the intermediate level.

```motoi
(on-frame
  (lambda ()
    (when (not (audio-playing?))
      (audio-play 'restart-cue))))
```

If the sound stops for any reason, kick it back on with a cue.
Write a novice Motoi example that uses `audio-rms`.

```motoi
(display (audio-rms))
```

RMS reads the average loudness — steadier than peak, better for slow visuals.
Show me `audio-rms` at the intermediate level.

```motoi
(on-frame
  (lambda ()
    (let ((energy (audio-rms)))
      (camera-scale (+ 1.0 (* 0.2 energy))))))
```

Breathe the camera in and out with the music's overall loudness.
Write an intermediate Motoi example that uses `audio-tempo`.

```motoi
(define (double-time)
  (audio-tempo (* 2 (or (audio-bpm) 120))))
(on-key
  (lambda (k)
    (when (eq? k 'd) (double-time))))
```

Press D to double whatever tempo the song is at right now.
Show me `audio-tempo` at the novice level.

```motoi
(audio-tempo 90)
```

Set playback tempo to 90 beats per minute — a walking pace.
Write a novice Motoi example that uses `audio-time`.

```motoi
(display (audio-time))
```

How long has the audio been playing? A single number in seconds.
Show me `audio-time` at the intermediate level.

```motoi
(on-frame
  (lambda ()
    (let ((t (audio-time)))
      (when (> t 30)
        (display "30 seconds in")))))
```

Trigger a moment once the song has been rolling for half a minute.
Write a novice Motoi example that uses `begin-frame`.

```motoi
(begin-frame 'main)
```

Start drawing on the surface called 'main. Pair with end-frame to publish.
Show me `begin-frame` at the intermediate level.

```motoi
(begin-frame 'card-a)
(grid-cell-set! 0 0 1)
(end-frame 'card-a)
```

Frame a small edit into card-a. Nothing shows on screen until end-frame.
Write a novice Motoi example that uses `camera-bring-to-view`.

```motoi
(define hero (entity/make 'player))
(camera-bring-to-view hero)
```

Move the camera so the hero is on screen with a bit of padding.
Show me `camera-bring-to-view` at the intermediate level.

```motoi
(define boss (entity/make 'boss))
(camera-bring-to-view boss 40 600)
```

Pan to the boss over 600 ms with 40 px of breathing room around them.
Write a novice Motoi example that uses `camera-center-on`.

```motoi
(camera-center-on 100 200)
```

Slide the camera so world point (100, 200) sits at the middle of the screen.
Show me `camera-center-on` at the intermediate level.

```motoi
(camera-center-on 0 0 2 400)
```

Center on the origin at 2x zoom, taking 400 ms to get there smoothly.
Write a novice Motoi example that uses `camera-export`.

```motoi
(define rec (camera-record))
(camera-export rec)
```

Turn a camera recording into a shareable GIF or image.
Show me `camera-export` at the intermediate level.

```motoi
(define rec (camera-record))
(camera-frame rec 'screen)
(camera-export rec '(kind gif fps 15))
```

Grab one screen frame, then export the whole recording as a 15-fps GIF.
Write an intermediate Motoi example that uses `camera-fit-all`.

```motoi
(on-key
  (lambda (k)
    (when (eq? k 'f)
      (camera-fit-all 20 800))))
```

Press F to fit every entity on screen over 800 ms, with 20 px padding.
Show me `camera-fit-all` at the novice level.

```motoi
(camera-fit-all 10)
```

Zoom out to fit everything with a 10 px margin around the group.
Write a novice Motoi example that uses `camera-frame`.

```motoi
(define rec (camera-record))
(camera-frame rec 'screen)
```

Snap the current screen into the recording as one frame.
Show me `camera-frame` at the intermediate level.

```motoi
(define rec (camera-record))
(on-frame
  (lambda ()
    (camera-frame rec 'screen)))
```

Grab every animation frame into the recording — good for making a smooth GIF.
Write an intermediate Motoi example that uses `camera-home`.

```motoi
(on-key
  (lambda (k)
    (when (eq? k 'h)
      (camera-home 500))))
```

Press H and glide back home over half a second.
Show me `camera-home` at the novice level.

```motoi
(camera-home 200)
```

Glide the camera back to (0, 0) over 200 ms.
Write a novice Motoi example that uses `camera-pan`.

```motoi
(camera-pan 50 0)
```

Shift the camera 50 units to the right without changing zoom.
Show me `camera-pan` at the intermediate level.

```motoi
(on-key
  (lambda (k)
    (cond ((eq? k 'left)  (camera-pan -20 0))
          ((eq? k 'right) (camera-pan 20 0))
          ((eq? k 'up)    (camera-pan 0 -20))
          ((eq? k 'down)  (camera-pan 0 20)))))
```

Arrow keys nudge the camera around. Classic level-editor controls.
Write a novice Motoi example that uses `camera-pan-to`.

```motoi
(camera-pan-to 200 100)
```

Slide the camera to an exact world position — the destination, not a delta.
Show me `camera-pan-to` at the intermediate level.

```motoi
(camera-pan-to 300 300 400)
```

Pan to (300, 300) over 400 ms. Good for cinematic reveals.
Write a novice Motoi example that uses `camera-record`.

```motoi
(define rec (camera-record))
```

Start a fresh recording. Feed it frames with camera-frame, then export.
Show me `camera-record` at the intermediate level.

```motoi
(define rec (camera-record))
(let loop ((i 0))
  (when (< i 30)
    (camera-frame rec 'screen)
    (wait 33)
    (loop (+ i 1))))
```

Grab 30 frames roughly one per 33 ms — about a second of footage.
Write a novice Motoi example that uses `camera-scale`.

```motoi
(camera-scale 2)
```

Zoom the camera to 2x. Everything looks twice as big.
Show me `camera-scale` at the intermediate level.

```motoi
(define (breathe)
  (on-frame
    (lambda ()
      (let ((t (audio-time)))
        (camera-scale (+ 1 (* 0.1 (sin t))))))))
```

Rock the zoom gently with a sine wave — a subtle living-scene effect.
Write a novice Motoi example that uses `camera-set!`.

```motoi
(camera-set! 100 100 1.5)
```

Place the camera at (100, 100) at 1.5x zoom in one step.
Show me `camera-set!` at the intermediate level.

```motoi
(define (snap-to hero)
  (let ((p (entity/pos hero)))
    (camera-set! (car p) (cadr p) 1)))
```

Snap the camera onto the hero without any smoothing — feels arcade-tight.
Write a novice Motoi example that uses `camera-state`.

```motoi
(display (camera-state))
```

Peek at where the camera is and how far it's zoomed.
Show me `camera-state` at the intermediate level.

```motoi
(define saved (camera-state))
(camera-fit-all)
(wait 1000)
(camera-set! (car saved) (cadr saved) (caddr saved))
```

Save the current view, take a wide-angle look for a second, then restore it.
Write a novice Motoi example that uses `camera-tilt`.

```motoi
(camera-tilt 0.1)
```

Rock the camera by a small angle. Just enough to feel a rumble.
Show me `camera-tilt` at the intermediate level.

```motoi
(on-frame
  (lambda ()
    (camera-tilt (* 0.05 (sin (audio-time))))))
```

Give the frame a soft rocking motion tied to the audio clock.
Write a novice Motoi example that uses `camera-trace`.

```motoi
(camera-trace 'on)
```

Turn on the trace overlay so you can see where the camera has been.
Show me `camera-trace` at the intermediate level.

```motoi
(camera-trace 'clear)
(camera-pan-to 200 0 500)
(camera-pan-to 0 200 500)
```

Clear the trace, then pan through two points. The trace shows the path.
Write a novice Motoi example that uses `camera-x`.

```motoi
(display (camera-x))
```

Read the camera's current world-x. Handy for saving positions.
Show me `camera-x` at the intermediate level.

```motoi
(on-frame
  (lambda ()
    (when (> (camera-x) 500)
      (camera-home))))
```

Snap home if the camera drifts past x=500. A gentle edge stop.
Write a novice Motoi example that uses `camera-y`.

```motoi
(display (camera-y))
```

Read the camera's current world-y.
Show me `camera-y` at the intermediate level.

```motoi
(define (below? y)
  (< (camera-y) y))
(if (below? 0) (display "upper") (display "lower"))
```

Decide whether the camera is looking at the upper half of the world.
Write a novice Motoi example that uses `camera-zoom-to`.

```motoi
(camera-zoom-to 3)
```

Zoom in to 3x. The scene fills more of the screen.
Show me `camera-zoom-to` at the intermediate level.

```motoi
(camera-zoom-to 0.5 600)
```

Zoom out to half-size over 600 ms for a slow reveal.
Write a novice Motoi example that uses `cancel-tick`.

```motoi
(define t (on-tick (lambda () (display "."))))
(cancel-tick t)
```

Register a tick handler, then quiet it back down with cancel-tick.
Show me `cancel-tick` at the intermediate level.

```motoi
(define counter 0)
(define t #f)
(set! t
  (on-tick
    (lambda ()
      (set! counter (+ counter 1))
      (when (>= counter 4)
        (cancel-tick t)))))
```

Self-cancelling ticker: prints four times then removes itself.
Write a novice Motoi example that uses `cols`.

```motoi
(display (cols))
```

How many columns wide is the current logical surface?
Show me `cols` at the intermediate level.

```motoi
(define (last-col) (- (cols) 1))
(display (last-col))
```

Compute the rightmost column index without hardcoding the width.
Write a novice Motoi example that uses `end-frame`.

```motoi
(end-frame 'main)
```

Finish the current frame on the 'main surface. Now the world can see it.
Show me `end-frame` at the intermediate level.

```motoi
(on-frame
  (lambda ()
    (begin-frame 'main)
    (grid-cell-set! 5 5 1)
    (end-frame 'main)))
```

A full render loop: open a frame, paint, close a frame.
Write a novice Motoi example that uses `grid-cell-age`.

```motoi
(display (grid-cell-age 3 4))
```

How many steps has cell (3, 4) been alive? Useful for coloring by age.
Show me `grid-cell-age` at the intermediate level.

```motoi
(define (old? cx cy)
  (> (grid-cell-age cx cy) 20))
(when (old? 5 5) (display "veteran cell"))
```
